title: DevFlow project config
description: DevFlow project configuration. The LOCAL tier (the /devflow:implement, /devflow:review, /devflow:docs skills you run inside Claude Code) works with NO config file at all — every value has a sane built-in default. You only need this file if you enable the GitHub Actions automation (the optional cloud/autopilot tier). See docs/cloud-setup.md.

$schema.description: Path/URL to this schema for editor autocomplete and validation. Ignored at load time.

base_branch.description: Branch that PRs target by default. Used as the review/merge base.

claude_model.description: Claude model for AI-powered workflows (individual workflows may override).

devflow.description: Shared DevFlow settings. allowed_bots/allowed_users gate triggering across ALL DevFlow workflows (devflow.yml, devflow-implement.yml, devflow-review.yml, devflow-runner.yml); workpad_marker is used by /devflow:implement; effort and allowed_tools apply specifically to the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml).

allowed_bots.description: Bot logins allowed to trigger DevFlow workflows (comma-separated). 'claude' is Anthropic's GitHub App identity; add your own bot/automation logins (e.g. dependabot).

allowed_users.description: Human logins allowed to trigger DevFlow command/implement workflows (comma-separated), in addition to the write/admin/maintain collaborator requirement. '*' (default) allows any collaborator; narrow it to restrict to specific logins. Bots are governed separately by allowed_bots.

workpad_marker.description: Marker line identifying the persistent /devflow:implement workpad comment on issues. One comment per issue whose body starts with this marker.

effort.description: Reasoning effort for the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml). 'medium' omits --effort so the model's built-in default applies.

allowed_tools.description: Extra --allowed-tools entries for the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml), APPENDED on top of devflow's built-in base list. Use claude-code-action tool syntax, e.g. "Bash(make:*)", "Bash(docker compose:*)". Does not apply to /devflow:implement — see devflow_implement.allowed_tools. Empty/omitted → base list only.

Extra --allowed-tools entries for the /devflow:implement path (devflow-implement.yml), APPENDED on top of devflow's built-in base list. The implement path does NOT inherit devflow.allowed_tools, so list every extra tool you want during implementation here. Empty/omitted → base list only.

devflow_runner.description: Settings for the reusable runner that backs the automated reviewer (devflow-review.yml → devflow.yml `runner` job).

allowed_tools.description: Extra --allowed-tools entries for the automated /devflow:review reviewer (devflow.yml `runner` job), APPENDED on top of the read-only review base profile. This is what lets a review actually BUILD/TEST a PR — e.g. "Bash(npm:*)", "Bash(npx:*)", "Bash(webpack:*)", "Bash(go:*)". /devflow:init auto-populates this per detected language. SECURITY: these are read from the BASE branch's committed config (never the PR head), so a PR cannot grant itself tools — but any build tool you list here runs the PR author's code (e.g. npm postinstall) on pull_request_target with a write token. Enable only tools you trust to run against untrusted PRs. Empty/omitted → read-only review only.

devflow_review_and_fix.description: Settings for /devflow:review-and-fix per-run subagent effectiveness telemetry. See docs/efficiency-trace.md.

efficiency_telemetry_enabled.description: When true, /devflow:review-and-fix derives a per-subagent effectiveness trace at Loop Exit, prints it to chat, and persists one per-run record to .devflow/logs/efficiency/. When false, the loop neither renders the trace nor writes any file under .devflow/logs/. Derivation is best-effort: a failure never aborts the fix loop.

efficiency_cut_candidate_min_dispatch.description: Minimum number of times a Phase-3 subagent must have been dispatched (across runs) before an all-null/noise agent is flagged as a cut candidate. Defined here so the config surface is stable; consumed by the follow-up cross-run analyzer, not by /devflow:review-and-fix itself. The per-run record carries this value forward for that analyzer.

setup.description: Cloud-tier runtime provisioning: how the GitHub Actions runner is prepared BEFORE Claude runs — for the /devflow:* command path, /devflow:implement, AND the automated reviewer. /devflow:init auto-populates node_version + install per detected language (deterministic presets), then the LLM enriches php_version/extensions and services by reading docker-compose/CI/composer.json. Provisioning order: Python → Node → PHP → service containers → install lines.

python_version.description: setup-python runs ONLY if non-empty. devflow's own helpers currently need Python >= 3.11 with PyYAML, so keep this set until devflow's bash-only refactor lands.

node_version.description: setup-node runs ONLY if non-empty. Set it (e.g. '20') if your project's tests/build need Node. When set, npm/yarn/pnpm download caching is enabled automatically IF a lockfile (package-lock.json / yarn.lock / pnpm-lock.yaml) is present at the repo root.

php_version.description: shivammathur/setup-php runs ONLY if non-empty (e.g. '8.3'). Sets the PHP runtime + Composer. Populated by /devflow:init from composer.json's require.php constraint when detectable.

php_extensions.description: CSV list of PHP extensions to install with setup-php (e.g. 'mbstring, pdo_mysql, redis, intl'). Only used when php_version is set. Populated by /devflow:init from composer.json require.ext-* and the project's service needs.

php_tools.description: CSV list of PHP tools for setup-php (e.g. 'composer:v2, phpunit'). Optional — Composer is set up by default. Only used when php_version is set.

services.description: Service containers (databases, caches, queues) started via `docker run -d` BEFORE the install lines, reachable from the runner on 127.0.0.1:<host-port>. NOT GitHub Actions `services:` (which can't be defined in a composite action or driven by config). Populated by /devflow:init from docker-compose.yml / .env / existing CI. The runner needs Docker (preinstalled on ubuntu-latest) and the `docker` preset's allowlist.

ports.description: Port mappings 'host:container', e.g. ['3306:3306']. The app's test config should connect to 127.0.0.1:<host-port>.

env.description: Environment variables passed as -e KEY=VALUE, e.g. {"MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD": "root", "MYSQL_DATABASE": "app_test"}.

options.description: Extra `docker run` options, one complete arg per array element (NOT a single space-separated string — that would word-split a health command's spaces). Use a health check so readiness is awaited, e.g. ["--health-cmd=mysqladmin ping -h 127.0.0.1", "--health-interval=5s", "--health-timeout=5s", "--health-retries=20"]. Maintainer-controlled, read from the trusted base config.

install.description: Shell lines run verbatim (joined with newlines) after the language setups and service-container start. Put devflow's own deps first, then your project's (e.g. 'composer install --no-interaction'). Empty array → skipped.

docs.description: Documentation paths used by the /devflow:docs suite.

documented_label.description: Label applied by /devflow:implement once its docs pass has run.

devflow_retrospective.description: DevFlow retrospective / self-improvement loop, consumed by the /devflow:retrospective-weekly orchestrator (a locally-run skill, not a GitHub Actions workflow).

retrospective_model.description: Optional --model override for the retrospective subagent.

audit_model.description: Optional --model override for the audit subagent.

implementation_branch_prefix.description: Branch prefix your implementation bot uses for its PRs. The loop only processes PRs on this prefix (plus DevFlow's own devflow/audit-* branches).

min_occurrences.description: Repetition gate. status == regressed always bypasses this.

cooldown_days.description: Skip audit if the youngest audit PR is younger than this (createdAt-based).

max_prs_per_run.description: Soft cap on PRs processed per scheduled retrospective run.

watched_authors.description: Authors whose merged PRs the loop retrospects. Defaults to devflow.allowed_bots if unset.

devflow.description: Enable the light /devflow:* command path (devflow.yml) and /devflow:implement (devflow-implement.yml).
